title: buddleja loricata
intro: Buddleja loricata, also known as the 'Karoo fogmaakierie' or 'Hairy-leaved Fountain Bush', is a shrub native to South Africa, belonging to the Buddleja genus of flowering plants. The leaves and flowers of Buddleja loricata are used in traditional medicine, and it is said to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and skin conditions, due to its potential health benefits. Herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, are made from the leaves and flowers of Buddleja loricata, which can be used to soothe coughs and other ailments.
